skill.name: electron
skill.description: Automate Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify, etc.) using agent-browser via Chrome DevTools Protocol. Use when the user needs to interact with an Electron app, automate a desktop app, connect to a running app, control a native app, or test an Electron application. Triggers include "automate Slack app", "control VS Code", "interact with Discord app", "test this Electron app", "connect to desktop app", or any task requiring automation of a native Electron application.
skill.summary: Automate Electron desktop apps using Chrome DevTools Protocol
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 57 potential issues, all are false positives from documentation examples. Shell commands are educational code snippets showing how to launch Electron apps with debugging flags and use agent-browser tool. No executable code, user input injection vectors, or malicious intent found. Skill is legitimate documentation for desktop automation.

content.value_statement: Control Electron apps like VS Code, Slack, Discord, and Figma through Chrome DevTools Protocol. Connect to running desktop applications, automate UI interactions, and test native Electron apps.
actual_capabilities: Connect to any Electron app via Chrome DevTools Protocol
Launch Electron apps with remote debugging enabled on macOS, Linux, and Windows
Take screenshots of desktop application windows
Inspect and interact with UI elements using element references
Extract text and data from desktop applications
Control multiple Electron apps simultaneously using named sessions
limitations: Requires Electron apps to be launched with --remote-debugging-port flag
Only works with Electron-based applications, not all desktop apps
App must be relaunched if already running to enable debugging port
Some apps use custom input components that may require alternative interaction methods

use_cases.title: Test Electron Applications
use_cases.description: QA engineers automate UI testing for Electron apps by simulating user interactions, verifying states, and capturing screenshots for documentation.

use_cases.title: Automate Workflows in Desktop Apps
use_cases.description: Developers automate repetitive tasks in Slack, Discord, or other Electron apps by connecting via CDP and programmatic control of UI elements.

use_cases.title: Extract Data from Native Apps
use_cases.description: Data analysts retrieve information from desktop applications like Notion or Obsidian by snapshotting app state and extracting structured data.

prompt_templates.title: Connect to Slack
prompt_templates.prompt: Launch Slack with remote debugging enabled, connect to it, and take a screenshot of the main window.
prompt_templates.scenario: Visual inspection of Slack app state

prompt_templates.title: Automate VS Code Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Connect to VS Code, snapshot the current window, and click on the Source Control icon to open the git panel.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Data from Notion
prompt_templates.prompt: Launch Notion with debugging port 9226, connect to it, and extract all text content from the current page using snapshot and get commands.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-App Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Connect to Slack on port 9222 and Discord on port 9224 using named sessions, then take screenshots of both apps simultaneously.

output_examples.input: Connect to Slack and list all interactive elements
output_examples.output: Connected to Slack on port 9222. Snapshot shows 47 interactive elements: @e1 (sidebar button), @e2 (channel list), @e10 (message input), @e15 (settings gear), @e20 (search box), @e25-@e40 (channel items), @e41-@e47 (toolbar buttons)

output_examples.input: Take a screenshot of the current Electron app window
output_examples.output: Screenshot captured: app-state.png (1920x1080 pixels, full window including all visible UI elements)

output_examples.input: Extract the text from the main content area
output_examples.output: Extracted text from @e5: 'Welcome to the workspace. You have 3 unread messages in #general and 5 mentions in random channels.'

best_practices: Quit the Electron app completely before relaunching with the --remote-debugging-port flag, as the flag must be present at launch time
Wait a few seconds after launching the app before connecting, as some apps take time to initialize their webview
Use agent-browser tab to list all targets if elements are not appearing, as Electron apps may have multiple windows or webviews
Use named sessions with --session flag when controlling multiple Electron apps simultaneously to avoid conflicts
anti_patterns: Try to connect to an already-running app that was launched without the debugging port - this will fail with connection refused
Assume all interactive elements are in the main window - many Electron apps use multiple webviews that require tab switching
Use click commands on elements without first taking a snapshot - you must snapshot to get valid element references
Ignore color scheme differences - connecting via CDP may default to light mode, so use --color-scheme dark to preserve dark mode

faq.question: Why do I get connection refused when trying to connect?
faq.answer: The app must be launched with the --remote-debugging-port flag. Quit the app completely, then relaunch it with the flag. Check that the port is not in use by another process.

faq.question: Can I automate any desktop application?
faq.answer: No, this only works with Electron-based applications. Any app built on Electron supports the --remote-debugging-port flag. Native Windows, macOS, or Linux apps without Electron are not supported.

faq.question: How do I find which port my Electron app is using?
faq.answer: You choose the port when launching the app with --remote-debugging-port=PORT. Common ports are 9222, 9223, 9224, etc. Each app needs a unique port if running multiple apps simultaneously.

faq.question: What if elements are not appearing in my snapshot?
faq.answer: The app may have multiple webviews. Use agent-browser tab to list all targets and switch to the correct one. Some elements may be in iframes or require navigation to become visible.

faq.question: Can I control multiple Electron apps at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, use named sessions with the --session flag. For example: agent-browser --session slack connect 9222 and agent-browser --session vscode connect 9223. Then interact with each independently.

faq.question: Why does the app look different when connected via CDP?
faq.answer: The default color scheme may be light mode. Use agent-browser --color-scheme dark snapshot -i to preserve dark mode, or set AGENT_BROWSER_COLOR_SCHEME=dark as an environment variable.
